original_abstract: As a core technology of Intelligent Transportation System, traffic flow prediction has a wide range of applications. The fundamental challenge in traffic flow prediction is to effectively model the complex spatial-temporal dependencies in traffic data. Spatial-temporal Graph Neural Network (GNN) models have emerged as one of the most promising methods to solve this problem. However, GNN-based models have three major limitations for traffic prediction: i) Most methods model spatial dependencies in a static manner, which limits the ability to learn dynamic urban traffic patterns; ii) Most methods only consider short-range spatial information and are unable to capture long-range spatial dependencies; iii) These methods ignore the fact that the propagation of traffic conditions between locations has a time delay in traffic systems. To this end, we propose a novel Propagation Delay-aware dynamic long-range transFormer, namely PDFormer, for accurate traffic flow prediction. Specifically, we design a spatial self-attention module to capture the dynamic spatial dependencies. Then, two graph masking matrices are introduced to highlight spatial dependencies from short- and long-range views. Moreover, a traffic delay-aware feature transformation module is proposed to empower PDFormer with the capability of explicitly modeling the time delay of spatial information propagation. Extensive experimental results on six real-world public traffic datasets show that our method can not only achieve state-of-the-art performance but also exhibit competitive computational efficiency. Moreover, we visualize the learned spatial-temporal attention map to make our model highly interpretable.
humanized_abstract: Traffic flow prediction is an essential part of the core technology of Intelligent Transportation System. Although there are a large number of studies that have explored traffic flow prediction in the past decade, one challenging issue is to model the underlying complex spatial-temporal patterns in traffic data. Recently, the application of spatial-temporal Graph Neural Network (GNN) in traffic flow prediction has become very popular, such as STSGNN, S-GCN, Graphwavenet and ST-GCN. While those methods have demonstrated their superior performance in terms of accuracy and speed, there are still many limitations for being applied in the real world, which are listed as follows: 1) Most existing methods rely heavily on the assumption that the spatial relationships of urban traffic network are static, which cannot keep up with changing traffic patterns; 2) Most graph-based models tend to focus only on the local connections within an urban traffic network, while overlooking the impact of distant locations; 3) The evolution of the local traffic conditions in an area is influenced not only by its neighboring areas, but also by those located in distant parts of the network, and therefore the traffic evolution between different graph locations is subject to certain lag or delay time. Therefore, it is of great necessity to design an efficient graph-based model which overcomes the aforementioned three shortcomings, and hence we propose a novel Propagation Delay-aware dynamic long-range transFormer, namely PDFormer, to make up for the existing shortcomings. Experiments based on six distinct real-world public traffic datasets are conducted, and the results show that the proposed PDFormer model significantly advances the state-of-the-art performance, meanwhile, the running speed is close to the leading models in spite of the much larger model size. Additionally, the attention map of our proposed model has been shown as the visualization proof to confirm the interpretability of the designed model.
